hypothesis: If open-source molecular generative models are pre-trained on large chemical datasets, fine-tuned on a curated CTF dataset, and subsequently optimized via reinforcement learning with expert-designed reward functions, then they will yield a higher fraction of valid, unique, diverse, and expert-filter–passing CTF candidates than models using pre-training alone or fine-tuning without RL because combined transfer learning and reward shaping align the models’ latent space with domain-specific structural constraints and objectives.
